 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-aho","title":"fcp.s3: AWS S3 Compatible Object Storage Connector","description":"# fcp.s3 - AWS S3 Compatible Object Storage Connector

## Overview
S3-compatible object storage connector supporting AWS S3, MinIO, Cloudflare R2, and other S3-compatible services.

## Archetype
**File/Blob**

## Key Operations

### Object Operations
- `s3.object.put` - Upload object
- `s3.object.get` - Download object
- `s3.object.head` - Get object metadata
- `s3.object.delete` - Delete object
- `s3.object.copy` - Copy object
- `s3.object.list` - List objects (with prefix, delimiter)
- `s3.object.list_versions` - List object versions

### Multipart Upload
- `s3.multipart.create` - Initiate multipart upload
- `s3.multipart.upload_part` - Upload part
- `s3.multipart.complete` - Complete upload
- `s3.multipart.abort` - Abort upload
- `s3.multipart.list_parts` - List uploaded parts

### Bucket Operations
- `s3.bucket.create` - Create bucket
- `s3.bucket.delete` - Delete bucket
- `s3.bucket.list` - List buckets
- `s3.bucket.exists` - Check bucket exists
- `s3.bucket.location` - Get bucket region

### Presigned URLs
- `s3.presign.get` - Generate presigned GET URL
- `s3.presign.put` - Generate presigned PUT URL
- Configurable expiration

### Metadata & Tags
- `s3.object.tagging.get/put/delete` - Object tags
- `s3.object.metadata` - Custom metadata

### Versioning
- `s3.versioning.get/put` - Bucket versioning
- `s3.object.versions.list` - List versions

## Providers Supported
- AWS S3
- MinIO
- Cloudflare R2
- DigitalOcean Spaces
- Backblaze B2
- Wasabi
- Custom S3-compatible endpoints

## Transfer Features
- Automatic multipart for large files
- Streaming upload/download
- Progress tracking
- Resume interrupted transfers
- Parallel part uploads

## Authentication
- AWS Signature V4
- IAM credentials
- STS temporary credentials
- Instance metadata (EC2)

## Rate Limits
- Per-prefix rate limiting
- 3,500 PUT/COPY/POST/DELETE per second per prefix
- 5,500 GET/HEAD per second per prefix

## Zone Architecture
```
Zone 0 (Public):     Public bucket contents
Zone 1 (Authorized): Private bucket (scoped)
Zone 2 (Private):    All buckets in account
Zone 3 (Sensitive):  AWS credentials
```

## Dependencies
- fcp-core, fcp-ratelimit, fcp-testkit, fcp-telemetry","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:48:10.705196543-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:35:09.983020834-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-aho","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-zx0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:16.000657925-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-aho","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-iv7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:16.029665486-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-aho","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-krt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:16.058032141-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ayc","title":"fcp.hubspot: Reporting and dashboards","description":"# fcp.hubspot - HubSpot CRM & Marketing Platform Connector

## Overview
Full HubSpot CRM and Marketing Hub connector for contact management, marketing automation, sales pipeline, and reporting.

## Archetype
**Hybrid: Request-Response + Webhook + Polling**

## Key Operations

### CRM Objects
- Contact, Company, Deal, Ticket CRUD
- Associations between objects
- Custom properties and objects
- Timeline activities

### Marketing Hub
- Email campaigns (create, send, analytics)
- Forms (submissions, analytics)
- Landing pages
- Marketing emails
- Workflows and automation

### Sales Hub  
- Pipeline management
- Sequences and templates
- Meetings scheduling
- Quotes and proposals

### Reporting & Analytics
- Dashboard access
- Custom reports
- Attribution reporting
- Revenue analytics
- Funnel metrics

### Integrations
- Webhook subscriptions
- App marketplace integration
- Custom integrations

## Dependencies
- fcp-core, fcp-oauth, fcp-ratelimit, fcp-testkit, fcp-telemetry","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:16:54.337905151-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:31:33.721994639-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T12:31:33.721994639-05:00","close_reason":"CONSOLIDATED: All HubSpot sub-features merged into comprehensive parent connector bead flywheel_connectors-hy8v (fcp.hubspot: HubSpot CRM & Marketing Platform Connector)"}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-bngd","title":"TEST: Discord Connector Integration Tests","description":"# TEST: Discord Connector Integration Tests

## Scope
Integration tests for the Discord connector using mocked Discord API.

## Test Categories

### Gateway WebSocket
- [ ] Gateway connection establishment
- [ ] IDENTIFY payload handling
- [ ] Heartbeat mechanism
- [ ] Session resumption (RESUME)
- [ ] Reconnection with backoff
- [ ] Gateway close codes handling

### REST API Operations
- [ ] Send message (various content types)
- [ ] Edit message
- [ ] Delete message
- [ ] Get channel info
- [ ] Get guild info
- [ ] Bulk message operations

### Event Handling
- [ ] MESSAGE_CREATE event parsing
- [ ] MESSAGE_UPDATE event parsing
- [ ] MESSAGE_DELETE event parsing
- [ ] GUILD_* events
- [ ] Event dispatch to subscribers

### Rate Limiting
- [ ] Per-route rate limit handling
- [ ] Global rate limit handling
- [ ] Bucket synchronization
- [ ] Retry-After header respect

### Error Scenarios
- [ ] Invalid token handling
- [ ] Permission denied errors
- [ ] Rate limit exhaustion
- [ ] Network failures
- [ ] Gateway disconnection

### FCP Protocol Compliance
- [ ] Handshake flow
- [ ] Capability validation
- [ ] Introspection accuracy
- [ ] Health check responses
- [ ] Metrics correctness

## Logging Requirements
- All WebSocket frames logged (debug level)
- API requests/responses logged with timing
- Error conditions logged with context
- Correlation IDs throughout

## Test Infrastructure
- wiremock for REST API mocking
- Custom WebSocket mock server for Gateway
- Configurable latency injection
- Error injection at any stage

## Coverage Target: >85%","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T12:31:50.840987336-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:32:35.520370339-05:00"}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-bvs","title":"Study: Clawdbot Connector Patterns - Synthesis & Recommendations","description":"# Study: Clawdbot Connector Patterns - Synthesis & Recommendations

## Objective

Synthesize findings from Discord and Telegram connector studies into actionable patterns and recommendations for FCP connector development.

## Synthesis Areas

### 1. Common Patterns Identified
- [ ] Shared architectural patterns
- [ ] Common abstractions
- [ ] Reusable utility functions
- [ ] Configuration patterns
- [ ] Error handling approaches

### 2. Platform-Specific Adaptations
- [ ] How patterns adapt to different APIs
- [ ] Platform-specific optimizations
- [ ] Unique challenges per platform
- [ ] Platform capability differences

### 3. Reusable Components Catalog

#### Core Infrastructure
- Connection management
- Retry/backoff utilities
- Rate limiter
- Event dispatcher
- Configuration loader

#### Message Processing
- Command parser
- Response builder
- Attachment handler
- Formatter utilities

#### State Management
- Session manager
- Cache layer
- Persistence adapter

### 4. Anti-Patterns to Avoid
- [ ] Identified code smells
- [ ] Scalability issues
- [ ] Security concerns
- [ ] Maintainability problems
- [ ] Testing difficulties

### 5. FCP Connector Design Recommendations

#### Architecture
- Recommended module structure
- Interface definitions
- Dependency injection patterns
- Plugin system design

#### Implementation Guidelines
- Coding standards
- Error handling strategy
- Logging requirements
- Testing requirements

#### Documentation Standards
- API documentation
- Configuration documentation
- Troubleshooting guides

## Deliverables

1. **FCP Connector Template** - Skeleton project with best practices
2. **Pattern Library** - Reusable patterns with examples
3. **Style Guide** - Coding and architecture standards
4. **Checklist** - Connector review checklist
5. **Migration Guide** - How to port existing connectors to FCP

## Success Criteria

- Clear, actionable recommendations
- Reusable code templates
- Documented patterns with rationale
- Identified gaps requiring new solutions
- Roadmap for connector development

## Dependencies

- Completion of Discord connector study
- Completion of Telegram connector study

## Time Estimate

- Synthesis: 3-4 hours
- Template creation: 2-3 hours
- Documentation: 2-3 hours
- Total: 7-10 hours","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:24:02.200654302-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:24:02.200654302-05:00"}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cdj","title":"fcp.1password: Credential Management Connector","description":"## Overview
Secure credential management connector for 1Password integration, enabling AI agents to safely access and manage secrets without exposing sensitive data.

## Core Capabilities

### Item Lookup (Read-Only by Default)
- Search vault items by name, tags, or categories
- Retrieve specific fields (username, URL, notes) without exposing passwords
- Read-only access as the default permission level
- Explicit elevation required for password retrieval

### Password Generation
- Generate strong passwords with configurable requirements
- Support for length, character sets, and complexity rules
- Memorable password generation (word-based)
- PIN generation for numeric-only contexts

### Entry Creation
- Create new login entries with generated passwords
- Support for secure notes, credit cards, identities
- Custom field support for application-specific data
- Automatic categorization and tagging

### Vault Organization
- List and navigate vault structure
- Move items between vaults
- Bulk tagging operations
- Archive and restore items

### Secure Note Access
- Read secure notes for configuration data
- Structured note parsing (YAML, JSON embedded)
- Document attachment retrieval
- Version history access

## Security Requirements

### Credential Protection
- **CRITICAL**: Never expose credentials in logs or output
- Mask all sensitive fields in debug output
- Use secure memory handling
- Clear credentials from memory after use

### Time-Limited Credential Leases
- Short-lived access tokens (5-minute default)
- Automatic credential rotation
- Lease renewal with re-authentication
- Audit trail for all credential access

## Implementation Notes
- Use 1Password CLI (op) or Connect API
- Support both personal and team accounts
- Service account integration for automation
- MFA handling for interactive sessions

## Testing Requirements
- Mock vault for unit tests
- Integration tests with test vault
- Security audit for credential handling
- Penetration testing for leak prevention","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:21:48.804778945-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:21:48.804778945-05:00"}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gif","title":"fcp.pinecone: Pinecone Vector Database Connector","description":"# fcp.pinecone - Pinecone Vector Database Connector

## Overview
Pinecone managed vector database connector for semantic search, recommendations, and AI applications.

## Archetype
**Database**

## Key Operations

### Index Operations
- `pinecone.index.create` - Create index
- `pinecone.index.describe` - Get index info
- `pinecone.index.list` - List indexes
- `pinecone.index.delete` - Delete index
- `pinecone.index.configure` - Update index config

### Vector Operations
- `pinecone.vectors.upsert` - Insert/update vectors
- `pinecone.vectors.query` - Similarity search
- `pinecone.vectors.fetch` - Fetch vectors by ID
- `pinecone.vectors.delete` - Delete vectors
- `pinecone.vectors.update` - Update vector metadata
- `pinecone.vectors.list` - List vector IDs

### Namespace Operations
- `pinecone.namespace.list` - List namespaces
- `pinecone.namespace.delete` - Delete namespace
- Per-namespace operations

### Collection Operations
- `pinecone.collection.create` - Create collection (backup)
- `pinecone.collection.list` - List collections
- `pinecone.collection.describe` - Get collection info
- `pinecone.collection.delete` - Delete collection

### Query Features
- Top-K similarity search
- Metadata filtering
- Include metadata/values options
- Sparse-dense hybrid search
- Namespace scoping

## Index Types
- Serverless (auto-scaling)
- Pod-based (dedicated resources)

## Metric Types
- cosine
- euclidean
- dotproduct

## Metadata Filtering
- $eq, $ne, $gt, $gte, $lt, $lte
- $in, $nin
- Compound filters ($and, $or)

## Authentication
- API key authentication
- Environment-based routing

## Rate Limits
- Per-plan limits
- Upsert: vectors per request limit
- Query: QPS limits

## Zone Architecture
```
Zone 0 (Public):     Index metadata
Zone 1 (Authorized): Query operations
Zone 2 (Private):    Vector data
Zone 3 (Sensitive):  API keys
```

## Dependencies
- fcp-core, fcp-ratelimit, fcp-testkit, fcp-telemetry","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:11:34.823043494-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:36:35.614054373-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gif","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-iv7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:43:27.852894723-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gif","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-krt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:43:27.887643529-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gif","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-zx0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T12:38:29.809998822-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gu3","title":"fcp.arxiv: Category and topic classification system","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:14:36.225098952-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:14:36.225098952-05:00"}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-h1wi","title":"TEST: Connector Performance Benchmark Suite","description":"# TEST: Connector Performance Benchmark Suite

## Overview
Performance benchmarks ensuring connectors meet FCP performance targets from AGENTS.md.

## Performance Targets (from spec)
| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Cold start | < 50ms |
| Request latency overhead | < 1ms |
| Memory footprint | < 10MB idle |
| Binary size | < 20MB (compressed) |

## Benchmark Categories

### Startup Benchmarks
- [ ] Cold start time measurement
- [ ] Warm start time
- [ ] Initialization overhead
- [ ] First request latency

### Latency Benchmarks
- [ ] Protocol overhead (JSON-RPC parsing)
- [ ] Capability validation overhead
- [ ] IPC round-trip time
- [ ] Introspection response time

### Memory Benchmarks
- [ ] Idle memory footprint
- [ ] Memory under load
- [ ] Memory leak detection (long-running)
- [ ] GC/allocation patterns

### Throughput Benchmarks
- [ ] Requests per second (single connector)
- [ ] Concurrent request handling
- [ ] Streaming throughput
- [ ] Event processing rate

### Resource Benchmarks
- [ ] CPU utilization at idle
- [ ] CPU under load
- [ ] File descriptor usage
- [ ] Network connection pooling

## Tooling
- criterion.rs for statistical benchmarks
- Custom harness for startup timing
- Memory profiling with jemalloc/heaptrack
- CI integration for regression detection

## Output
- JSON benchmark results
- Historical comparison
- Regression alerts (>10% degradation)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T12:13:06.779412276-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:21:36.285666797-05:00"}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-h32","title":"STANDARD: Connector Testing Requirements and Quality Gates","description":"# STANDARD: Connector Testing Requirements and Quality Gates

## Overview
Defines mandatory testing requirements and quality gates that ALL FCP connectors must pass before release.

## Unit Test Requirements

### Coverage Thresholds
- Overall: >80% line coverage
- Critical paths: >95% coverage
- Security code: >98% coverage

### Required Test Categories
- [ ] All FcpConnector trait methods
- [ ] Configuration validation (valid/invalid)
- [ ] Handshake success/failure scenarios
- [ ] Capability token validation
- [ ] Each operation (success, common errors, edge cases)
- [ ] Rate limit handling
- [ ] Timeout behavior
- [ ] Error recovery

### Mocking Requirements
- NEVER make real API calls in unit tests
- Use wiremock or similar for HTTP mocking
- Mock external service responses
- Test error injection (network, auth, rate limit)

## Integration Test Requirements
- [ ] Real handshake flow (with test credentials)
- [ ] Basic operation execution
- [ ] Event subscription lifecycle
- [ ] Reconnection handling

## E2E Test Requirements
- [ ] Cold start performance (<50ms)
- [ ] Protocol compliance verification
- [ ] Security boundary enforcement
- [ ] Resource usage within limits

## Quality Gates (CI/CD)
1. `cargo check --all-targets` - No errors
2. `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` - No warnings
3. `cargo fmt --check` - Properly formatted
4. `cargo test` - All tests pass
5. `cargo test --release` - Release tests pass
6. Coverage threshold met
7. `ubs` scan clean
8. No security vulnerabilities

## Documentation Requirements
- [ ] All public APIs documented
- [ ] AI hints for each operation
- [ ] Configuration schema documented
- [ ] Error codes documented","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:38:48.045099693-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:23:22.579821039-05:00"}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-h35","title":"fcp.arxiv: Paper search with semantic and keyword modes","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:14:32.145747042-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:14:32.145747042-05:00"}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hc2","title":"fcp.cron: Scheduled Task Runner","description":"# Scheduled Task Runner Meta-Connector

**Force Multiplier:** Time-based automation for FCP zones.

## Overview

Many automation workflows require scheduled execution: daily reports, periodic syncs, cleanup jobs, reminder systems. This meta-connector provides a robust scheduling infrastructure that triggers FCP actions on time-based schedules.

## Core Components

### 1. Cron Expression Scheduling
- Standard cron syntax (5-field and 6-field)
- Extended syntax with seconds precision
- Timezone-aware scheduling
- Named schedules for common patterns
- Cron expression validation and preview

```
# Standard patterns
@hourly    = 0 * * * *
@daily     = 0 0 * * *
@weekly    = 0 0 * * 0
@monthly   = 0 0 1 * *

# Custom patterns
*/15 * * * *     = Every 15 minutes
0 9 * * 1-5      = 9 AM on weekdays
0 0 1,15 * *     = 1st and 15th of month
```

### 2. One-Time Scheduled Tasks
- Schedule task for specific datetime
- Support for relative scheduling ("in 30 minutes")
- Cancellation before execution
- Rescheduling capability
- Batch scheduling (multiple one-time tasks)

### 3. Interval-Based Triggers
- Fixed interval execution (every N minutes/hours/days)
- Sliding window intervals
- Jitter support for load distribution
- Minimum interval enforcement
- Pause/resume capability

### 4. Task History and Logs
- Complete execution history
- Stdout/stderr capture
- Execution duration tracking
- Success/failure status
- Searchable log storage
- Log retention policies

### 5. Failure Alerting
- Configurable alert channels (email, Slack, PagerDuty)
- Alert on:
  - Task failure
  - Task timeout
  - Consecutive failures
  - Missed schedules
- Alert suppression/snooze
- Escalation policies

## Configuration Example

```toml
[[schedules]]
name = "daily-backup"
cron = "0 2 * * *"
timezone = "America/New_York"
action = "zone.backup.run_backup"
params = { full = true }
timeout = "1h"
alert_on_failure = true

[[schedules]]
name = "hourly-sync"
interval = "1h"
jitter = "5m"
action = "zone.sync.pull_updates"
retry_on_failure = 3

[[one_time]]
name = "migration-task"
run_at = "2024-02-01T03:00:00Z"
action = "zone.db.run_migration"
params = { version = "v2.0" }
```

## Task State Machine

```
PENDING → RUNNING → COMPLETED
              ↓
           FAILED → RETRYING → COMPLETED/FAILED
              ↓
         DEAD (max retries exceeded)
```

## Dashboard Features

- Calendar view of scheduled tasks
- Next N upcoming executions
- Recent execution history
- Live execution monitoring
- Manual trigger capability
- Bulk operations (pause all, resume all)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Sub-second scheduling precision
- [ ] Support 1000+ concurrent schedules
- [ ] Missed task detection and alerting
- [ ] Distributed execution (no single point of failure)
- [ ] Complete audit trail
- [ ] Hot-reload of schedule configurations

## Dependencies

- Persistent storage for schedules and history
- Time synchronization (NTP)
- Alert channel integrations
- Zone action invocation system

## Considerations

- Handle daylight saving time transitions
- Cluster-safe (only one instance executes)
- Graceful shutdown (complete running tasks)
- Clock skew tolerance
- Long-running task support","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:23:20.104073834-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:35.225906099-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:35.225906099-05:00","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-hiu (fcp.cron: Scheduled Task Runner Connector)"}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-k4ka","title":"fcp-graphql: GraphQL Client Infrastructure Library","description":"# fcp-graphql: GraphQL Client Infrastructure Library

## Overview
Shared GraphQL client library for connectors that interface with GraphQL APIs (Linear, GitHub, Notion, Shopify, etc.).

## Features

### Core Client
- Async GraphQL client with connection pooling
- Query/mutation/subscription support
- Automatic retry with exponential backoff
- Request batching and deduplication
- Query complexity estimation

### Query Building
- Type-safe query builder
- Fragment composition
- Variable handling
- Directive support

### Pagination
- Cursor-based pagination (Relay-style)
- Offset-based pagination
- Automatic page traversal
- Total count estimation

### Subscriptions
- WebSocket transport
- Reconnection handling
- Subscription multiplexing
- Heartbeat management

### Introspection
- Schema introspection caching
- Type validation
- Deprecation warnings

### Error Handling
- GraphQL error parsing
- Partial data handling
- Network error recovery
- Rate limit detection

## Dependencies
- reqwest (HTTP)
- tokio-tungstenite (WebSocket)
- serde_json
- fcp-core

## Consumers
- fcp.linear (GraphQL-only API)
- fcp.github (GraphQL API v4)
- fcp.notion (REST+GraphQL hybrid)
- fcp.shopify (Admin API)

## Testing
- Mock GraphQL server
- Response recording/playback
- Schema validation tests","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T12:10:53.615224102-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:11:35.210477464-05:00"}

## Overview
Comprehensive observability library providing structured logging, metrics collection, and distributed tracing for FCP connectors.

## Structured Logging
- JSON output format
- Log level filtering (trace → error)
- Automatic field injection:
  - Correlation ID
  - Zone ID
  - Connector ID
  - Request ID
- Sensitive data redaction
- Async log writing

## Metrics Collection
- Counter metrics (requests, errors)
- Gauge metrics (connections, queue depth)
- Histogram metrics (latencies, sizes)
- Timer helpers
- Label/tag support
- Pre-defined FCP metrics

## Distributed Tracing
- Span creation and nesting
- Context propagation (W3C Trace Context)
- Trace ID generation
- Span attributes
- Sampling strategies
- Cross-connector correlation

## Export Formats
- Prometheus exposition format
- OTLP (OpenTelemetry Protocol)
- JSON logs to stdout
- Custom sinks

## Integration
- tracing crate integration
- HTTP middleware for auto-instrumentation
- FCP operation decoration
- Health endpoint exposure

## Performance
- Low overhead (<1% CPU)
- Async export
- Batch flushing
- Sampling support

## Dependencies
- tracing, tracing-subscriber
- opentelemetry, opentelemetry-otlp
- metrics, fcp-core","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:37:41.061916979-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:31:12.611225229-05:00"}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ptr","title":"fcp.anthropic - Anthropic AI Provider Connector","description":"# FCP Anthropic Connector - Anthropic AI Provider

## Overview
A comprehensive connector for Anthropic's Claude API, enabling agents to leverage Claude models for complex reasoning, tool use, vision capabilities, and batch processing with built-in cost tracking and budget enforcement.

## Priority: P1 (Critical Infrastructure)

## Core Capabilities

### 1. Messages API
- Send messages to Claude models
- Streaming responses for real-time output
- Multi-turn conversations
- System prompts
- Model selection (claude-opus-4-5-20251101, claude-sonnet-4-20250514, claude-haiku, etc.)
- Temperature and sampling controls
- Max tokens configuration
- Stop sequences

### 2. Tool Use Support
- Define tools with JSON schema
- Handle tool_use responses
- Execute tools and return results
- Multi-tool conversations
- Parallel tool execution
- Tool choice forcing (auto, any, specific)

### 3. Vision Capabilities
- Images in prompts (base64 or URL)
- Multiple images per message
- Image analysis and description
- Document/screenshot understanding
- Chart and diagram interpretation

### 4. Prompt Caching
- Cache system prompts
- Cache large context documents
- Cache tool definitions
- Automatic cache management
- Cache hit/miss tracking
- Cost savings calculation

### 5. Batch API
- Submit batch requests
- Track batch status
- Retrieve batch results
- Cancel batches
- Cost-efficient bulk processing
- 50% cost reduction for batch jobs

### 6. Cost Tracking & Budget Enforcement
- Per-request cost calculation
- Running total tracking
- Budget limits per agent/session
- Alerts at thresholds
- Automatic cutoff at budget limit
- Cost attribution and reporting

## FCP Integration

### Manifest Structure
```toml
[connector]
name = "fcp.anthropic"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Anthropic Claude API integration with cost tracking"

[capabilities]
messages = { risk = "medium", description = "Send messages to Claude" }
streaming = { risk = "medium", description = "Stream responses" }
tool_use = { risk = "medium", description = "Use tools in conversations" }
vision = { risk = "medium", description = "Process images" }
batch = { risk = "low", description = "Submit batch requests" }

[trust_zones]
default = "z:ai:inference"

[resources]
rate_limit_rpm = 1000
rate_limit_tpm = 100000
default_timeout_ms = 120000

[billing]
track_costs = true
budget_enforcement = true
```

### Tool Definitions
```yaml
tools:
  - name: anthropic_message
    description: Send a message to Claude
    parameters:
      model: 
        type: string
        enum: [claude-opus-4-5-20251101, claude-sonnet-4-20250514, claude-3-5-haiku-20241022]
        required: true
      messages:
        type: array
        items:
          type: object
          properties:
            role: { type: string, enum: [user, assistant] }
            content: { type: string }
        required: true
      system: { type: string }
      max_tokens: { type: integer, default: 4096 }
      temperature: { type: number, default: 1.0, min: 0, max: 1 }
      stream: { type: boolean, default: false }

  - name: anthropic_message_with_tools
    description: Send a message with tool definitions
    parameters:
      model: { type: string, required: true }
      messages: { type: array, required: true }
      tools:
        type: array
        items:
          type: object
          properties:
            name: { type: string }
            description: { type: string }
            input_schema: { type: object }
      tool_choice: 
        type: object
        properties:
          type: { type: string, enum: [auto, any, tool] }
          name: { type: string }

  - name: anthropic_message_with_vision
    description: Send a message with images
    parameters:
      model: { type: string, required: true }
      messages:
        type: array
        items:
          type: object
          properties:
            role: { type: string }
            content:
              type: array
              items:
                oneOf:
                  - type: object
                    properties:
                      type: { const: text }
                      text: { type: string }
                  - type: object
                    properties:
                      type: { const: image }
                      source:
                        type: object
                        properties:
                          type: { enum: [base64, url] }
                          media_type: { type: string }
                          data: { type: string }

  - name: anthropic_batch_create
    description: Create a batch request
    parameters:
      requests:
        type: array
        items:
          type: object
          properties:
            custom_id: { type: string }
            params: { type: object }
        required: true

  - name: anthropic_batch_status
    description: Get batch status
    parameters:
      batch_id: { type: string, required: true }

  - name: anthropic_batch_results
    description: Get batch results
    parameters:
      batch_id: { type: string, required: true }

  - name: anthropic_get_usage
    description: Get current usage and costs
    parameters:
      period: { type: string, enum: [session, day, month] }
```

## Cost Tracking

### Pricing Model (as of implementation)
| Model | Input (per 1M tokens) | Output (per 1M tokens) |
|-------|----------------------|------------------------|
| claude-opus-4-5-20251101 | $15.00 | $75.00 |
| claude-sonnet-4-20250514 | $3.00 | $15.00 |
| claude-3-5-haiku | $0.25 | $1.25 |

### Budget Enforcement
```rust
struct BudgetConfig {
    max_per_request: Option<f64>,
    max_per_session: Option<f64>,
    max_per_day: Option<f64>,
    warning_threshold: f64,  // 0.0 - 1.0
}
```

### Cost Attribution
- Track costs per agent
- Track costs per task/session
- Track costs per tool
- Generate reports

## Prompt Caching Strategy
- System prompts > 1024 tokens: Always cache
- Tool definitions: Cache when > 5 tools
- Large documents: Cache with TTL
- Cache warming on connector init

## Error Handling
- Rate limit (429): Exponential backoff
- Overloaded (529): Queue and retry
- Invalid request (400): Parse and report
- Auth failure (401): Refresh credentials
- Context length exceeded: Truncation strategy

## Dependencies
- anthropic-rs or custom HTTP client
- reqwest (HTTP)
- tokio (async)
- serde (serialization)
- async-stream (streaming)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Send basic message and receive response
- [ ] Stream response tokens in real-time
- [ ] Use tools in conversation
- [ ] Process images in prompts
- [ ] Submit and retrieve batch jobs
- [ ] Track costs accurately per request
- [ ] Enforce budget limits
- [ ] Handle rate limits gracefully
- [ ] Utilize prompt caching","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:11:55.294212353-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:42:07.614456761-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:42:07.614456761-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Anthropic Claude API connector with Messages API, streaming support, cost tracking, and unit tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ptr","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-zx0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:43:18.580791794-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ptr","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-iv7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:43:18.616615002-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ptr","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-krt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:43:18.65424983-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ptr","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9co","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:43:18.687755696-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qtu","title":"fcp.grafana: Open-Source Observability Connector","description":"## Overview
Comprehensive observability connector for Grafana ecosystem, enabling AI agents to query metrics, analyze logs, inspect traces, and manage dashboards.

## Core Capabilities

### Metrics Querying (PromQL)
- Execute PromQL queries against Prometheus datasources
- Time range selection with relative and absolute times
- Aggregation and transformation functions
- Multi-datasource queries
- Query result formatting (tables, time series)

### Dashboard Access and Rendering
- List available dashboards by folder/tag
- Retrieve dashboard JSON definitions
- Render dashboard panels as images
- Variable interpolation for dynamic dashboards
- Export dashboards for backup/migration

### Alert Management
- List active and silenced alerts
- Query alert history and state transitions
- Create and modify alert rules
- Manage silence rules
- Acknowledge and annotate alerts

### Log Search (Loki Integration)
- LogQL query execution
- Log stream discovery
- Label-based filtering
- Pattern matching and parsing
- Log context retrieval around events

### Trace Inspection (Tempo Integration)
- Trace ID lookup and visualization
- Service map generation
- Span analysis and timing breakdown
- Cross-service correlation
- Trace-to-log linking

## Deployment Support

### Self-Hosted
- Direct API authentication
- Custom SSL/TLS configuration
- On-premise datasource access
- Air-gapped environment support

### Grafana Cloud
- Cloud API token authentication
- Stack management
- Usage metrics and billing info
- Multi-tenant organization support

## Implementation Notes
- Use Grafana HTTP API
- Support API key and OAuth authentication
- Handle rate limiting gracefully
- Cache frequently accessed dashboards

## Testing Requirements
- Mock Grafana server for unit tests
- Integration tests with test instance
- Performance tests for large queries
- Compatibility tests across Grafana versions","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:21:49.552569516-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:21:49.552569516-05:00"}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-quo","title":"fcp.youtube - YouTube Data & Studio API Connector","description":"# fcp.youtube - YouTube Data & Studio API Connector

## Overview
Full YouTube integration covering video discovery, transcript extraction, analytics, and content management for media-rich Flywheel workflows.

## Archetype
**Hybrid: Request-Response + Polling**
- Request-Response: Data API for search, metadata, comments
- Polling: Analytics updates, processing status checks

## Key Operations

### Video Operations
- `youtube.video.get` - Get video metadata by ID
- `youtube.video.list` - List videos with filters
- `youtube.video.search` - Search videos with query
- `youtube.video.update` - Update video metadata (own videos)
- `youtube.video.delete` - Delete video
- `youtube.video.rate` - Like/dislike video
- `youtube.video.report_abuse` - Report video

### Transcript Operations
- `youtube.transcript.get` - Get video transcript
- `youtube.transcript.list` - List available transcript tracks
- `youtube.transcript.auto` - Get auto-generated captions
- `youtube.transcript.manual` - Get manually uploaded captions
- `youtube.transcript.translate` - Get translated transcript
- `youtube.caption.upload` - Upload caption track
- `youtube.caption.update` - Update caption track
- `youtube.caption.delete` - Delete caption track

### Channel Operations
- `youtube.channel.get` - Get channel by ID
- `youtube.channel.list` - List channels (by username, etc.)
- `youtube.channel.mine` - Get authenticated user's channel
- `youtube.channel.sections` - Get channel sections
- `youtube.channel.branding` - Get branding settings

### Playlist Operations
- `youtube.playlist.create` - Create playlist
- `youtube.playlist.update` - Update playlist metadata
- `youtube.playlist.delete` - Delete playlist
- `youtube.playlist.list` - List playlists
- `youtube.playlist.items.list` - Get playlist videos
- `youtube.playlist.items.add` - Add video to playlist
- `youtube.playlist.items.remove` - Remove from playlist
- `youtube.playlist.items.reorder` - Reorder playlist items

### Comment Operations
- `youtube.comment.list` - List video comments
- `youtube.comment.create` - Post comment
- `youtube.comment.reply` - Reply to comment
- `youtube.comment.update` - Edit comment
- `youtube.comment.delete` - Delete comment
- `youtube.comment.moderate` - Set moderation status
- `youtube.comment.threads` - Get comment threads

### Analytics Operations (YouTube Analytics API)
- `youtube.analytics.query` - Query analytics data
- `youtube.analytics.reports` - Generate reports
- `youtube.analytics.realtime` - Real-time view data
- `youtube.analytics.demographics` - Audience demographics
- `youtube.analytics.traffic` - Traffic sources
- `youtube.analytics.engagement` - Engagement metrics

### Upload Operations (Resumable Upload)
- `youtube.upload.init` - Initialize resumable upload
- `youtube.upload.chunk` - Upload video chunk
- `youtube.upload.status` - Check upload status
- `youtube.upload.resume` - Resume interrupted upload
- `youtube.upload.finalize` - Complete upload
- `youtube.upload.thumbnail` - Set video thumbnail

### Live Streaming Operations
- `youtube.live.broadcast.create` - Create broadcast
- `youtube.live.broadcast.bind` - Bind to stream
- `youtube.live.broadcast.transition` - Change broadcast state
- `youtube.live.stream.create` - Create live stream
- `youtube.live.chat.messages` - Get live chat messages
- `youtube.live.chat.send` - Send chat message

## Quota Management
YouTube API has complex quota system:
- Read operations: 1-100 units
- Write operations: 50-1600 units
- Daily quota: 10,000 units default

### Quota Strategy
- Query cost pre-calculation
- Quota budget allocation per workflow
- Caching layer to reduce redundant calls
- Batch requests where possible
- parts parameter optimization

## Zone Architecture
```
Zone 0 (Public):     Public video metadata, search results
Zone 1 (Authorized): User watch history, subscriptions
Zone 2 (Private):    Analytics data, unlisted videos
Zone 3 (Sensitive):  OAuth tokens, API keys
```

## Dependencies
- `fcp-core`: Base connector traits
- `fcp-oauth`: Google OAuth 2.0 flow
- `fcp-upload`: Resumable upload handling
- `fcp-cache`: Metadata caching

## Integration Points
- **Flywheel Media**: Video content indexing
- **Flywheel Knowledge**: Transcript ingestion for RAG
- **fcp.openai**: Transcript summarization
- **fcp.whisper**: Alternative transcription

## Implementation Notes
- Use YouTube Data API v3
- Implement exponential backoff for quota errors
- Cache video metadata (TTL: 1 hour)
- Support both API key and OAuth authentication
- Handle regional restrictions gracefully
- Implement proper ISO 8601 duration parsing
- Support snippet, contentDetails, statistics parts
- Handle live vs. uploaded vs. premiere video types","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:12:42.750344186-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:12:42.750344186-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-quo","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lj2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T12:38:14.808711852-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-quo","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-zx0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T12:38:14.841162305-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-quo","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-krt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T12:38:14.872945622-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-s4c4","title":"TEST: Telegram Connector Integration Tests","description":"# TEST: Telegram Connector Integration Tests

## Scope
Integration tests for the Telegram connector using mocked Bot API.

## Test Categories

### Polling Mechanism
- [ ] getUpdates long polling
- [ ] Update offset tracking
- [ ] Timeout configuration
- [ ] Polling restart on error
- [ ] Graceful shutdown during poll

### Bot API Operations
- [ ] sendMessage (text, formatting)
- [ ] sendPhoto/sendDocument
- [ ] editMessageText
- [ ] deleteMessage
- [ ] getMe (bot info)
- [ ] getChat/getChatMember

### Message Handling
- [ ] Text message parsing
- [ ] Command parsing (/command)
- [ ] Entity extraction (mentions, URLs, etc.)
- [ ] Reply handling
- [ ] Media message handling

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","title":"fcp.github: GitHub API Connector","description":"# fcp.github - GitHub API Connector

## Overview
Comprehensive GitHub integration supporting repositories, issues, PRs, actions, and webhooks for developer workflow automation.

## Archetype
**Hybrid: Request-Response + Webhook + GraphQL**

## Key Operations

### Repository Operations
- `github.repo.get/list/create/update/delete` - Repository management
- `github.repo.contents.get/create/update/delete` - File operations
- `github.repo.branches.list/get/create/delete` - Branch management
- `github.repo.tags.list` - Tag listing
- `github.repo.releases.list/get/create` - Release management
- `github.repo.collaborators.list/add/remove` - Access control

### Issue Operations
- `github.issue.create/get/update/close` - Issue CRUD
- `github.issue.list` - List with filters (state, labels, assignee)
- `github.issue.comments.list/create/update/delete` - Issue comments
- `github.issue.labels.add/remove` - Label management
- `github.issue.assignees.add/remove` - Assignee management

### Pull Request Operations
- `github.pr.create/get/update/close/merge` - PR lifecycle
- `github.pr.list` - List with filters
- `github.pr.reviews.list/create/submit` - Code review
- `github.pr.comments.list/create` - PR comments
- `github.pr.files.list` - Changed files
- `github.pr.commits.list` - PR commits
- `github.pr.checks.list` - Check runs

### Actions & Workflows
- `github.actions.workflows.list/get/dispatch` - Workflow management
- `github.actions.runs.list/get/cancel/rerun` - Workflow runs
- `github.actions.jobs.list/get/logs` - Job details
- `github.actions.artifacts.list/download` - Build artifacts

### Search Operations
- `github.search.repositories` - Repository search
- `github.search.code` - Code search
- `github.search.issues` - Issue/PR search
- `github.search.users` - User search

### User & Organization
- `github.user.get/authenticated` - User info
- `github.org.list/get` - Organization info
- `github.org.members.list` - Org members
- `github.org.teams.list/get` - Team management

### Webhook Operations
- `github.webhook.create/delete/list` - Webhook management
- `github.webhook.deliveries` - Delivery history
- Signature verification (X-Hub-Signature-256)

## API Support
- **REST API v3** - Primary for most operations
- **GraphQL API v4** - Complex queries, mutations
- **Webhooks** - Real-time event notification

## Rate Limiting
- 5,000 requests/hour (authenticated)
- 60 requests/hour (unauthenticated)
- GraphQL: 5,000 points/hour
- Secondary limits for search/creates

## Authentication
- OAuth 2.0 (web flow)
- Personal Access Token
- GitHub App (JWT + installation tokens)

## Zone Architecture
```
Zone 0 (Public):     Public repo metadata, public user info
Zone 1 (Authorized): Private repos (user scope)
Zone 2 (Private):    Organization data, team info
Zone 3 (Sensitive):  OAuth tokens, app credentials
```

## Dependencies
- fcp-core, fcp-oauth, fcp-graphql, fcp-ratelimit, fcp-webhook, fcp-testkit, fcp-telemetry","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:55:27.085625247-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:33:40.848016725-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lj2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:55:38.855121869-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-zx0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:55:39.111456972-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-vi7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:55:39.356814226-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-iv7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:55:39.642260144-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-krt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:55:39.909275648-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-k4ka","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T12:12:02.902007028-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-shj4","title":"TEST: fcp-oauth Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-oauth Unit Test Suite

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for the OAuth2/OAuth1 authentication library.

## Test Categories

### OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Flow
- [ ] Authorization URL generation with state/PKCE
- [ ] Token exchange with valid/invalid codes
- [ ] Refresh token flow
- [ ] Token expiration handling
- [ ] Scope validation

### OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials Flow
- [ ] Token acquisition
- [ ] Caching and refresh

### OAuth 1.0a Flow (Twitter/legacy)
- [ ] Request token acquisition
- [ ] User authorization redirect
- [ ] Access token exchange
- [ ] Signature generation (HMAC-SHA1)

### Token Management
- [ ] Token storage/retrieval
- [ ] Token validation
- [ ] Secure token handling (no disk writes)

### Error Handling
- [ ] Invalid client credentials
- [ ] Expired tokens
- [ ] Revoked tokens
- [ ] Network failures
- [ ] Rate limits

## Mocking Strategy
- Use wiremock to mock OAuth provider endpoints
- Test against mock Google, GitHub, Twitter, Slack endpoints
- Inject errors at each stage of flows
